commit.message:
Add steps action, quiet mode, and compact snapshots for token efficiency

Three optimizations targeting the remaining ~60% token waste from
auto-snapshots attached to every browser action:

1. steps action: Execute multiple actions in one call with a single
   snapshot at the end. Calendar navigation (click next month x2 +
   click day) goes from 3 tool calls + 3 snapshots to 1 call + 1
   snapshot. Max 20 steps per call. Supports all action types except
   nested steps/download/wait.

2. quiet mode: Append ' quiet' to target or set value='quiet' on
   click/press/scroll/clear/back to suppress the auto-snapshot
   entirely. For when the LLM already knows the next action.

3. Compact snapshots: Reduce element cap from 80 to 50 and text
   preview from 3000 to 1500 chars in LookAsync. Cuts ~40% of
   snapshot tokens without losing important information.

Also updates tool descriptions and system prompt with efficiency
best practices emphasizing steps and quiet mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix browser_js async bug and expand element detection

Live testing revealed two critical issues:

1. browser_js returned empty '{}' for ALL scripts because the async
   IIFE wrapper caused WebView2's ExecuteScriptAsync to return the
   Promise object immediately instead of the resolved value. Fixed by
   using a synchronous IIFE wrapper. Promise-returning scripts now get
   a helpful message instead of silent failure.

2. Element detection missed MUI radio buttons, custom checkboxes,
   comboboxes, and calendar grid cells (role=radio, role=checkbox,
   role=combobox, role=gridcell, etc.). Expanded the interactive
   element CSS selector and extracted it as a constant to avoid
   the 7-copy duplication that existed before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Drastically improve browser form automation for React/Vue/Angular sites

Major overhaul of browser automation tools based on analysis of a real
chat session where booking restaurant reservations failed due to
framework-incompatible form interaction (1131 tool calls, 282 reasoning
blocks, most reservations failed).

Core fix - Framework-aware type action:
- Replace naive el.value=text with native setter trick used by
  Playwright and React Testing Library
- Use Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set
  to bypass React/Vue/Angular value tracking
- Reset el._valueTracker so React detects the change
- Dispatch InputEvent (not just Event) with proper data/inputType fields
- Add blur event dispatch to trigger form validation
- Handle both input and textarea elements with respective prototype setters

New browser_do actions:
- fill: Batch fill multiple form fields in one call via JSON mapping,
  eliminating per-field snapshots that wasted enormous context tokens
- read_form: Read all form fields with names, values, types, required
  status, validation errors, and element indices in a single call
- clear: Clear a field using the framework-aware approach

Improved existing actions:
- select: Now handles custom dropdown components (react-select, MUI,
  downshift) by clicking to open then clicking the matching option,
  not just native <select> elements. Uses async IIFE for proper
  Promise handling.
- browser_js: Wrap user scripts in try/catch, return structured error
  messages instead of silently returning null. Handle undefined/null
  returns explicitly. Support Promise-returning scripts.

System prompt updates:
- Document all new actions with examples
- Add form filling best practices (read_form -> fill -> verify)
- Guide LLM to use fill for batch operations instead of per-field type
- Advise moving on quickly from CAPTCHA/CC-blocked platforms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: chat scroll anchoring - prevent forced scroll, ensure content visibility

Three fixes for scroll/anchoring issues:

1. When tracking latest tail (wasMountedToLatestTail), trim HEAD pages
   instead of TAIL when exceeding MaxMountedPages. Previously,
   TrimMountedTailOverflow removed the newest content, making tool
   calls and messages invisible after long assistant turns.

2. Add EnsureLatestMounted() to TranscriptWindowController and call it
   from OnUserMessageSent. When the user scrolls up and sends a message,
   the mounted window now re-mounts the latest pages so the sent message
   is visible.

3. Add TrimMountedHeadOverflow() counterpart to TrimMountedTailOverflow()
   for symmetric page trimming from either end.

Includes 5 new unit tests covering the fixed scenarios.

commit.message:
fix(training): make BestModelCallback save PTL-compatible checkpoints (#831)

Fixes two-phase training resume via trainer.fit(ckpt_path=...).

Previously, checkpoint_best_total.pth could not be loaded via PTL's
ckpt_path parameter because:
1. The file lacked the state_dict and loops keys PTL expects.
2. PyTorch >=2.6 defaulted torch.load to weights_only=True, rejecting
   the pickled TrainConfig Pydantic object stored under "args".

Source fix (no adapter needed):
- BestModelCallback now saves args as a plain dict (model_dump()) so
  weights_only=True works without registering safe globals.
- Adds PTL-compatible keys to every saved .pth: state_dict (model.
  prefixed), loops (epoch counter via _make_fit_loop_state), global_step,
  and pytorch-lightning_version.
- strip_checkpoint preserves these PTL keys so checkpoint_best_total.pth
  also works with ckpt_path.
- _make_fit_loop_state and _ckpt_args_get helpers moved/added to
  state_dict.py; readers (detr.py, module_model.py) use _ckpt_args_get
  to handle both dict (new) and namespace/object (legacy) args.
- checkpoint_io.py removed (no longer needed).

---------

fix: use absolute positioning for copy button in code blocks (#684)

* fix: use absolute positioning for copy button in code blocks

The copy button was using `float: right` which caused it to take up
space in the document flow, resulting in an extra empty line appearing
at the bottom of code blocks.

Switch to `position: absolute` with `right: 1rem` and `bottom: 1rem`
to overlay the button without affecting the code block layout. This
also adds `position: relative` to `pre` elements to establish the
positioning context.

Refs: https://github.com/nodejs/doc-kit/pull/635

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace the floating copy button with a dedicated toolbar at the bottom
of code blocks that includes a language label and copy button. This
resolves the visual issue where the copy button caused an empty-line
appearance at the bottom of code blocks.

Changes:
- Add createToolbarElement() to generate toolbar with language label
- Update copy button click handler to use closest('pre') for robust
  DOM traversal
- Add .code-toolbar flex layout and .code-language styling

commit.message:
fix(api): surface backbone_lora/freeze_encoder in ModelConfig (#829)

* fix(api): surface backbone_lora/freeze_encoder in ModelConfig; warn on ignored device kwarg

Three improvements from the API structure review:

1. Promote `backbone_lora` and `freeze_encoder` to `ModelConfig` (both
   `bool = False`) so power users can configure them via the public API.
   `_namespace.py` now reads them from `mc` instead of hardcoded defaults.
   `gradient_checkpointing` was already in `ModelConfig`.

2. Emit `DeprecationWarning` in `RFDETR.train()` when `device=` is passed
   with a non-"cpu" value.  Previously the kwarg was silently dropped and PTL
   auto-selected the device, which was invisible to callers migrating from the
   legacy API.  `"cpu"` is still honoured (maps to `accelerator="cpu"`).
   Test updated: `test_device_kwarg_silently_dropped` split into
   `test_device_kwarg_cpu_no_warning` + `test_device_kwarg_non_cpu_emits_deprecation`.

3. Tag all `TODO(Chapter 6)` comments in `module_model.py` and `module_data.py`
   with tracking issue #828.

Replace the intermediate SimpleNamespace layer in RFDETRDataModule and
RFDETRModelModule with direct reads from model_config / train_config.
Builder functions (build_model, build_criterion_and_postprocessors,
build_dataset, get_param_dict, validate_checkpoint_compatibility) still
receive a locally-scoped namespace via build_namespace() — build_namespace
itself is preserved as it is consumed by detr.py and auto_batch.py.

- Delete _build_args() and self._args from both modules
- Replace self._args.X with self.model_config.X / self.train_config.X
- Handle num_classes mutation in _load_pretrain_weights as local var

chore: ignore obsidian and img junction

Add .obsidian/ and img/ to .gitignore for local Obsidian compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(datasets): silently drop degenerate `bboxes` before Alb. val (#825)

COCO annotations sometimes place a bounding box exactly on the image
boundary, resulting in x_min == x_max (or y_min == y_max) after
coordinate clipping. Albumentations' check_bboxes rejects these with
"x_max is less than or equal to x_min", crashing DataLoader workers.

Filter out zero-width/zero-height boxes in _apply_geometric_transform
before passing to Albumentations. Labels, idxs, and masks are updated
in lockstep; because idxs carries original indices, downstream
_filter_per_instance_fields still slices per-instance fields correctly
from the un-filtered target.

Adds two regression tests covering the bbox-only and bbox+masks cases.

commit.message:
Fix infer backward refinement and refresh test goldens (#61)

* Rename array copy to copy_from, fix ident crash on unresolved names, fix prefix FuncName(args) parsing, cown load not readonly

* Dot pass: bind FuncName as zero-arg call when at end of expression (a.method FuncName)

* Deduplicate wrapper primitives using IRSubtype.invariant after alias/shape resolution

* Infer: default-typed literals yield to concrete types at merge points

When merging types from multiple control flow paths (e.g., match join),
a default-typed literal (from an unresolved Const) now yields to any
concrete type from a non-default path. This allows match arms with bare
integer literals to adopt the type from the else arm or downstream
field assignment, enabling literal refinement across match boundaries.

* WIP: DefaultInt/DefaultFloat literal type inference - removes is_default field

Infrastructure complete: DefaultInt/DefaultFloat tokens, is_default() method,
resolve(), merge_type handling, extract_primitive, end-of-pass sweep.
Existing tests pass. Iowise match-arm inference not yet working (WF issue
with TypeName leaking into Const type position during Phase 2).

* WIP: DefaultInt/DefaultFloat - preserve in Const nodes, defer lambda-calling parents for Phase 2

* WIP: DefaultInt - fix sweep for Const/Type contexts, fix extract_primitive, debug reveals merge ordering issue

* WIP: DefaultInt - fix merge_type to only yield to compatible primitives, add New arg default refinement

* WIP: DefaultInt - cascade from New, root cause identified: need cross-function propagation in Phase 2

* WIP: DefaultInt - Phase 2 cross-function propagation attempt (not yet working)

* Lambda grafting: inline lambda inference in parent, cascade refines match arm literals

Lambda apply functions are processed inline by their parent function's
infer convergence loop, sharing the same env. The New arg cascade
refines DefaultInt Consts in grafted lambda bodies to the field type.
Match arm literals like (out) -> 1 assigned to fd: i32 are now
correctly refined to i32.

* Fix: process lambda apply functions both independently and inline

Lambda apply functions need independent processing (for When/cown
return types) AND inline processing (for parent cascade integration).
Removing the Phase 1 skip restores when-lambda behavior while keeping
match-arm cascade refinement working.

* Cleanup: remove dead Phase 2 cross-function code, guard run_cascade

Remove ~200 lines of dead cross-function propagation code from Phase 2
that was superseded by lambda grafting. Add performance guard on
run_cascade to skip when no default Consts exist in env. Restore
DefaultInt reset in Phase 2 for lambda return type re-inference.

* Cache lambda ClassDef lookups in process_function

Build a lambda_cache map at the start of the label loop instead of
traversing the body and scope on every CallDyn. O(1) lookup replaces
O(N) traverse per lambda call per convergence iteration.

* Replace env_fingerprint string construction with direct env comparison

Remove expensive string serialization + sorted map + node cloning from
the Phase B convergence check. Replace with direct env_changed()
comparison that short-circuits on pointer equality. ~20% improvement
on the match_value_infer test (23s → 19s).

* Optimize run_cascade: index-based queue propagation

Replace O(iterations * labels * statements) full-rescan cascade with
O(changed_locations * fanout) index-based queue propagation. Build
reverse index (source location → statements) once per process_function,
reuse across all cascade calls.

* Use Subtype for type comparison in infer pass

- Move DefaultInt/DefaultFloat token definitions to lang.h
- Add DefaultInt/DefaultFloat axioms (AxiomEq + no-contradiction) to subtype.h
- Replace ad-hoc types_equal() with Subtype.invariant() throughout infer.cc
- Remove fast_merge_type(); Phase B uses merge_type() with init_env skip optimization
- Remove debug timing instrumentation
- 10x speedup on match_value_infer (15s -> 1.5s)

* Simplify infer pass: table-driven handlers, consolidated post-convergence, delta envs

- Table-driven dispatch for 35 trivial statement handlers (4 tables replace ~120 lines of copy-paste else-if branches)
- Delete unused merge_env function
- Consolidate 3 post-convergence label iterations (Const finalization, NewArrayConst finalization, TypeAssertion cleanup) into one pass
- Extract finalize_const lambda to deduplicate Const finalization between main and lambda paths
- Delta branch_exits: store only narrowed entries instead of full env copies
- Delta exit_envs: store only entries that differ from init_env baseline
- Net reduction: 4425 -> 4293 lines (-132)

* Fix infer_backward regression, rename match_type_error to match_value_match, regenerate golden files

- Fix Phase 4 backward refinement: check call_node instead of is_default()
  for triggering backward refinement of prior calls. The call result type
  may be a concrete wrapper (e.g., wrapper[u64]) even when the inference
  was from default-typed literals.
- Rename match_type_error to match_value_match: with DefaultInt inference,
  the match arm literal is correctly inferred as i32, so the test now
  succeeds instead of producing a type error.
- Regenerate all golden files for DefaultInt/DefaultFloat changes.
- 210/210 compile+run tests pass.

* Fix generic_when: only graft lambda bodies that have default-typed literals

Lambda grafting processes lambda bodies inline during the parent function's
process_label_body. This enables bidirectional type refinement for DefaultInt
literals. However, grafting can pollute the lambda's AST with stale
intermediate types from the parent's env, causing incorrect return type
inference when the lambda is later processed independently.

Fix: only graft when the lambda body actually contains DefaultInt/DefaultFloat
Const nodes that need refinement from the parent context. Lambdas without
default literals (like match-arm lambdas in generic when blocks) are left
to their own process_function for correct independent type inference.

212/212 compile+run tests pass.

* Remove lambda grafting, improve When param propagation and TypeArgs re-inference

- Remove CallDyn lambda grafting (inline lambda body processing). It
  modified the original lambda's AST, causing incorrect return types
  for lambdas in generic contexts. The deferred loop handles
  cross-function type propagation instead.
- Remove lambda_cache infrastructure (no longer needed).
- When handler: unconditionally set lambda params from cown types
  (not just when TypeVar). This ensures params have concrete types
  when the deferred loop re-processes the lambda.
- TypeArgs re-inference: when existing TypeArgs contain TypeParam
  references (from a prior pass with generic types), re-infer them
  from actual arg types. This enables lambda classes created with
  generic args to get concrete TypeArgs after cown type propagation.
- has_typevar: check for DefaultInt/DefaultFloat Const nodes in
  function body, not just return type. Ensures when-lambda bodies
  with unresolved literals are deferred for re-processing.

212/212 compile+run tests pass. generic_when still fails (pre-existing
issue requiring generic lambda instantiation in the deferred loop).

* Remove incorrect When return type propagation from enclosing function

The When handler was propagating the enclosing function's return type
to the when-lambda's apply. This is wrong: the when-lambda's return
type T determines the When result as cown[T], and T must be computed
from the lambda's body, not from the enclosing function.

In generic contexts, the lambda body may contain unresolved method
calls on TypeParams. The infer pass correctly leaves the return as
TypeVar for the reify pass to resolve after monomorphization.

generic_when still fails due to a reify pass function processing order
issue: lambda::apply is processed before lambda::apply's return
type is resolved.

* Add infer pass design document

Bidirectional dataflow analysis with per-label environments and merge-based
type lattice. Replaces Phase A/A.5/B, lambda grafting, and cascade with a
single fixpoint loop. Each statement has a transfer function with forward
and backward components, all using merge. ~1080 lines estimated vs 4280
current.

* Final infer pass design doc with implementation prompt

Complete rewrite guide for the next session. Includes:

commit.message:
fix(mps): eliminate MPS CPU fallbacks for `grid_sample` and bicubic backward (#821)

- `grid_sampler_2d_backward`: backward pass of `F.grid_sample` not yet
  implemented on MPS.  Replaced with `_bilinear_grid_sample` in
  `utilities/tensors.py` — gather-based index arithmetic that is native
  on every backend.  Non-MPS devices delegate to `F.grid_sample` unchanged
  (CUDA fused kernel preserved).  Affects deformable attention fallback
  (`ms_deform_attn_core_pytorch`) and segmentation point sampling
  (`point_sample`).
- `_upsample_bicubic2d_aa_backward`: anti-aliased bicubic backward not
  implemented on MPS.  Conditionally disable `antialias` on MPS in both
  DINOv2 position embedding interpolation paths (`dinov2.py`,
  `dinov2_with_windowed_attn.py`).

Adds 35 parity tests in `tests/utilities/test_tensors.py` verifying the
manual bilinear path matches `F.grid_sample` numerically (atol=1e-5) and
passes `torch.autograd.gradcheck`.

- Move padding_mode validation before shape unpack for fail-fast behaviour
- Cast bilinear weights to input.dtype to preserve float16 on MPS
- Fix mislabelled bicubic comment in dinov2.py
- Update stale point_sample docstring to reference _bilinear_grid_sample
